articles.title: Yogi Orders FIR Over Remarks on Akhilesh’s Daughter, Says No Insult Against Women Is Acceptable
articles.description: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has condemned derogatory comments made against Samajwadi Party president Akhilesh Yadav’s daughter, Aditi Yadav, and said such remarks against any daughter are unacceptable. Addressing a gathering in Azamgarh, Yogi stated that he personally instructed police to register an FIR as soon as the matter came to his notice. While defending Aditi, the Chief Minister also called on Akhilesh Yadav to ensure his supporters maintain decorum and avoid inflammatory language. The incident has sparked a fresh political row and renewed discussion on the limits of political discourse in the digital age.

articles.title: Growing public dissatisfaction against Karnataka govt will be reflected in 2028 election: BJP
articles.description: The national president of the BJP, Nitin Nabin, will decide on any change in the leadership of the party’s Karnataka unit, the party's National General Secretary and Rajya Sabha member Radha Mohan Das Agrawal said.

articles.title: 'Lethal actions not justified': Jaishankar to US over killing of Indian sailors; Rubio says 'blockade violation won't be tolerated'
articles.description: India US Tension: EAM S Jaishankar lodges strong protest with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio over the killing of three Indian mariners in the Gulf.

articles.title: Violations of U.S. blockade in Strait of Hormuz will not be tolerated: Rubio tells Jaishankar
articles.description: Rubio warns against U.S. blockade violations in the Strait of Hormuz, emphasizing compliance for peace and security.

articles.description: Sena (UBT) MPs summoned to Mumbai, Uddhav meet on Sunday

articles.title: On PMPML buses, women say overcrowding, harassment and inaction travel together
articles.description: Nehalika Chavan, a human rights officer, recalled a case involving one of her clients, a young girl who regularly commuted by bus.

articles.title: Yogi Govt Forms SIT To Probe 'Missing Donation' Allegations After Ram Temple Trust's Request
articles.description: The SIT team has been directed to submit a preliminary report within seven days and a final report within 15 days.

articles.title: 'All vessels should comply with orders from US forces in Hormuz', Rubio tells Jaishankar
articles.description: 'All vessels should comply with orders from US forces in Hormuz', Rubio tells Jaishankar

articles.title: PM Modi Heads to France for G7 Summit Amid West Asia Crisis | CNNNews18 Decodes What's on the Agenda
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has departed for France to attend the 52nd G7 Summit in Évian from June 15 to 17 amid escalating tensions in West Asia. The summit is expected to focus on pressing global challenges, including regional security, economic resilience, energy security, artificial intelligence, supply chains, and geopolitical stability. n18oc_world News18 Mobile App - https://onelink.to/desc-youtube

articles.title: ‘Violations will not be tolerated’: Rubio responds after Jaishankar protests attacks on ships carrying Indian sailors
articles.description: Jaishankar spoke to Rubio on Friday, hours after MEA summoned US Chargé d'Affaires Jason Meeks for the second time in less than 48 hours over attacks involving commercial vessels carrying Indian crew.

articles.title: Sejal Pawar Faces 15-Day Suspension Over Her ‘Male Cadavers’ Joke At Pranit More’s Show
articles.description: Pawar had been in the limelight for her remarks about comparing the sizes of male cadavers' genitalia while interacting with the audience during Pranit More's show.

articles.title: Wife named relatives in cruelty case; court says vague allegations cannot drag relatives into matrimonial disputes
articles.description: The Madras High Court ruled that Section 498A charges require specific allegations, not just marital discord or familial connections. Relatives were cleared in a case where the wife's claims lacked concrete details against them. The court emphasized that criminal proceedings should not be used for revenge, separating genuine grievances from vague accusations.

articles.title: Cessation of employment on unauthorised absence not illegal termination, says Supreme Court
articles.description: The Supreme Court has ruled that an employer’s decision to treat an employee’s prolonged unauthorised absence as cessation of service does not amount to illegal termination if the workman fails to provide any documentary proof of intimation or justification.

articles.title: Gym trainers need to undergo police verification, carry ID at all times in Ghaziabad
articles.description: The Ghaziabad district administration mandates police verification and identity cards for gym trainers to enhance safety and accountability.

articles.title: President Murmu reviews IMA passing out parade; first batch of nine women officers join Army
articles.description: President Murmu reviews IMA passing out parade; first batch of nine women officers join Army

articles.title: 'Unacceptable': UP CM condemns remarks against Akhilesh Yadav's daughter
articles.description: The remarks came after alleged online trolling and objectionable comments against Yadav's daughter Aditi Yadav led to complaints by the Samajwadi Party and affiliated organisations

articles.title: Uganda challenges US-led travel restrictions amid Ebola scare
articles.description: Uganda criticises Ebola travel bans, saying restrictions are unfair despite limited cases and a widely praised outbreak response.

articles.title: Parsamaniya Garhi: The Historic Fort At The Centre Of Nagod Royal Family Feud
articles.description: Parsamaniya Garhi is a royal estate linked to the erstwhile Nagod royal family.

articles.title: Sabarimala appointments row: Devaswom Board retains officials despite High Court warning
articles.description: The Travancore Devaswom Board has retained key Sabarimala officials, including an Executive Officer linked to past controversies, despite the High Court's call for greater vigilance in sensitive appointments

articles.title: Indian seafarers' deaths, disputed attacks and a widening US-Iran blame game
articles.description: India watches closely as the US and Iran trade accusations over maritime incidents. Both nations present conflicting accounts of negotiations and military achievements. These disputes directly impact India, with its seafarers caught in the middle and its energy security at stake. The war over narratives is as crucial as battlefield developments.

articles.title: Mamata Banerjee's Toughest Political Test Yet? TMC Rebels Make Their Move
articles.description: A group of 19 TMC MPs are all set to ask the Speaker to recognise their faction as the real party. If this happens, Mamata Banerjee might find herself out of the party she founded. This has also raised questions about who will get the party's ₹220 crore assets.

articles.title: US-Iran peace deal likely in Geneva on June 14: Check 5 key agenda points
articles.description: Nuclear limits, sanctions relief and Hormuz reopening headline a possible US-Iran breakthrough.

articles.title: Removing Trump name from Kennedy Center: Why is there a delay? Critics complain ‘something ain’t right'
articles.description: President Donald Trump's name was set to be removed from the Kennedy Center, but a 12-hour delay has been reportedly been sought by the administration.

articles.title: K Annamalai gets rousing welcome in Coimbatore after leaving the BJP
articles.description: K Annamalai, former BJP Tamil Nadu president, received a warm welcome in Coimbatore after quitting the party. He plans to launch a new political party, with his "We The Leader" movement gaining significant online support from his followers.

articles.title: JMM seeks Uttarakhand-like online voter tools for Jharkhand polls
articles.description: Jharkhand Mukti Morcha (JMM) wrote to the ECI, urging for online voter search and booth mapping facilities in Jharkhand. The party seeks to replicate Uttarakhand's model to enhance voter accessibility, transparency, and participation in elections.

articles.title: BCB Condemns Harassment of Cricketer Nayeem Hasan, Demands Probe
articles.description: The BCB has condemned the harassment of national cricketer Nayeem Hasan by law enforcement in Chattogram, demanding a full investigation. The board is in contact with the player and authorities to ensure his well-being and find a resolution.

articles.title: Tell Me Why? The Bull’s-Eye Is Within
articles.description: In a parliamentary democracy, public opinion on political parties is best reflected in elections. Kerala voters, known for their extraordinary political consciousness, delivered their verdict in black and white in the April 9 assembly election....

articles.title: 'India Will Give Voice To Global South': PM Modi Departs For 6-Day Visit To France, Slovakia
articles.description: PM Modi will participate in the G7 Summit being held in Evian, France, from June 15 to 17, marking the eighth consecutive summit where India has been invited.

articles.title: Rahul Gandhi's approach does not strengthen INDIA alliance: Pinarayi Vijayan
articles.description: Kerala Opposition Leader Pinarayi Vijayan has criticized Congress leader Rahul Gandhi. Vijayan stated Gandhi's political approach does not strengthen the INDIA alliance. He believes it often helps the BJP. Vijayan mentioned other non-Congress parties also raised objections during a meeting. He feels Gandhi's actions create a situation that benefits the BJP's interests.

articles.title: Even more than unity, Opposition needs a paradigm shift in its politics
articles.description: When the people are on the street expressing their disquiet and anger against the government, the Opposition must treat this as an opportunity, not as a threat

articles.title: 'In National Interest': 20 Rebel MPs To Meet Lok Sabha Speaker To Stake Claim As 'Asli TMC', Back NDA
articles.description: A faction of around 20 rebel TMC MPs has announced their support for the BJP-led NDA, citing national interest. Suspended TMC leader Riju Dutta indicated that a meeting with Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla is planned.

articles.title: Abhishek's Peace Offer After Kalyan's 'Choose Him Or Me' Ultimatum To Mamata Banerjee
articles.description: Amid a deepening crisis within the TMC, Abhishek Banerjee struck a conciliatory tone after Kalyan Banerjee's blistering attack, seeking to defuse tensions as rebellion and factionalism rock the party.

articles.title: Fund diversion case: Chandigarh Smart City officer Nalini Malik, bizman Wadhwa among 7 named in CBI’s 1st chargesheet
articles.description: Apart from Malik and Wadhwa, former IDFC officials Ribhav Rishi, Abhay Kumar and Seema Dhiman and two unnamed bank officials also figure in the chargesheet.

articles.title: $3 Billion Transfer to Iran? UAE Rejects Reports as 'Unfounded' and 'False'
articles.description: The UAE denied reports of a $3 billion transfer to Iran as "false and unfounded," stating no frozen funds were released, and urged media to rely on verified, official sources.

articles.title: Trump accuses Iran of attacking Indian ships; Tehran rejects charge
articles.description: Iran has vehemently rejected Trump's allegation, calling it 'baseless'

articles.title: Judge rejects bid to halt removal of Trump name from Kennedy Center
articles.description: WASHINGTON: A US federal judge on Friday (Jun 12) rejected a bid by the board of the Kennedy Center and the Justice Department to halt the removal of President Donald Trump's name from the performing arts v

articles.title: 19 signatories, 20 serial numbers: Mystery over 20th TMC rebel as party heads for split
articles.description: Fresh developments indicate momentum building up ahead of the TMC splitting, with the rebels succeeding in their effort to be recognised as the party.

articles.title: Punjab: Arvind Kejriwal declares Bhagwant Mann as CM face, hints at early elections
articles.description: Kejriwal also highlighted the AAP government’s welfare initiatives, including 300 units of free electricity for domestic consumers and the recently launched Mukh Mantri Sehat Yojana, under which health treatment worth up to Rs 10 lakh has been made available free of cost.

articles.title: Assam govt's first month: Key decisions & Nagaland border deal sealed
articles.description: The new Assam government marks its first month with a historic tripartite agreement to resolve the Assam-Nagaland border dispute. It also initiated an EU collaboration for a Blue Valley Cluster and undertook several infrastructure and welfare projects.

articles.title: Iran's FM Araghchi says draft US deal to be signed 'remotely', possibly in 'coming days'
articles.description: Araghchi had earlier said that a framework dubbed the "Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding" aimed at ending the war with the United States which broke out on February 28 "has never been closer."

articles.title: Spencer Pratt concedes LA mayor race, claims he has damaging video of a candidate
articles.description: PRATT'S CONCESSION SPEECH: "You think you can get rid of me that easily? I didn't get in this for political power. I got in to expose this corrupt machine, and nothing has changed."

articles.title: US-Iran MoU to cover nuclear deal, sanctions, Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: Iran's foreign minister outlined a two-stage MoU with the US, covering the nuclear program, sanctions relief, and the Strait of Hormuz. Key demands include lifting the US naval blockade, unfreezing assets, and US respect for Iran's sovereignty.

articles.title: Trump 'Humiliated' As Kennedy Center Starts Removing His Name After Crushing Court Defeat
articles.description: Workers have begun removing Donald Trump’s name from Washington’s iconic Kennedy Center after a federal judge ruled that the venue was illegally renamed in his honor. The court found that only Congress has the authority to change the institution’s official name, delivering a major legal setback for Trump and his allies. Crews were seen setting up scaffolding as the center rushed to comply with the ruling, while references to Trump were removed from signage, documents, and online materials. The dispute has sparked intense debate over presidential influence, cultural institutions, and the future identity of one of America’s most prominent arts venues.

articles.title: ‘Potato UFO’ Over Secret US Sites? Pentagon’s New Dump EXPOSES Strange Sightings Caught On Cam
articles.description: The Pentagon has released a third batch of declassified UFO files, containing dozens of documents, videos, and reports dating back decades. The records detail mysterious aerial sightings investigated by U.S. agencies around the world, including strange glowing orbs and unexplained objects. However, officials say the files still contain no conclusive proof of extraterrestrial life.

articles.title: Samajwadi Party banks on PDA formula, 'soft Hindutva' for 2027 UP Assembly polls
articles.description: PDA Formula: Samajwadi Party is relying on its PDA strategy and soft Hindutva outreach to counter BJP in Uttar Pradesh ahead of the 2027 Assembly elections, shaping alliances, caste mobilisation and electoral narratives across key constituencies UP politics

articles.title: BJP bets on Yogi Adityanath, Hindutva for hat-trick in 2027 UP Assembly polls
articles.description: Up Elections: BJP is focusing on Hindutva mobilisation and organisational consolidation as Yogi Adityanath prepares for a third term bid in 2027 Uttar Pradesh assembly polls amid shifting voter dynamics and opposition alliances electoral narrativeUP

articles.title: Iran: Araghchi says draft deal covers Strait of Hormuz, Lebanon conflict
articles.description: Iran: Araghchi says draft deal covers Strait of Hormuz, Lebanon conflict

articles.title: Trump Administration Appeals Court Ruling Striking Down $100,000 H-1B Visa Fee
articles.description: The Trump administration is appealing a federal court decision that deemed its $100,000 fee on H-1B visa applications unlawful.

articles.title: Trump Claims Iran Deal, Tehran Disagrees: Ex-Diplomat Suresh Goel
articles.description: Former diplomat Suresh Goel notes the contradiction between Trump's claim of a US-Iran deal and Tehran's denial. He suggests both sides seek a temporary truce for their own interests and calls the recent tanker attack that killed Indians a war crime.
